name: Cefalothin
executiveSummary: First-generation cephalosporin. Surgical prophylaxis, treatment of respiratory tract, middle ear, skin and soft tissue infections caused by susceptible bacteria: gram-positive cocci.
indications:
## Main indications

First-generation cephalosporin. Surgical prophylaxis, treatment of respiratory tract, middle ear, skin and soft tissue infections caused by susceptible bacteria: gram-positive cocci.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Powder vial containing 1 g (Arecemin, Cefalotina Larjan, Drawer, Dosagluc, Cefade, Richet).
adulto.diluent: 1–2 g in 100 mL 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: Yes. Reconstitute with 10 mL SWFI, 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose and administer over 3–5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute 1 g in 100 mL 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose and administer over 30–60 min.
adulto.notes: If vial contents do not dissolve completely, add SWFI (0,2–0,5 mL) and shake vigorously. Darkening of the solution at room temperature does not affect efficacy. IM administration is possible.

pediatrico.dose: 100 mg/kg/day every 6–8 h. Adults: 500–2000 mg every 6 h. Preoperative dose in adults: 1–2 g. Maximum dose: 12 g.
pediatrico.notes: Concomitant use with aminoglycosides may increase risk of nephrotoxicity.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 12 h at room temperature, 96 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 24 h at room temperature.

## Pediatric guide

- 96 h refrigerated; 12 h at room temperature.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Allergic reactions, thrombophlebitis, thrombocytopenia, leukopenia, nephrotoxicity.
